title: Vehicle Stability Assist (VSA) system structure description - Hill start assist control

blocks.text: Vehicle Stability Assist (VSA) system structure description - Hill start assist control

When starting on a slope with a steep gradient, the brake pressure control temporarily suppresses the descent that occurs when the driver changes from the brake pedal to the accelerator pedal to assist the driver's starting operation. The hill start assist control may not be activated when the brake pedal is not pressed hard enough when stopping or when the brake pedal is released gradually when starting off.

After determining that the vehicle has stopped from all wheel sensor signals, the inclination angle is calculated from the front and rear G signals of the G sensor, and the target hydraulic pressure is calculated according to the inclination angle.

When a change in the pressure sensor signal indicates that the driver has released the brake, the brake fluid pressure is held at the target pressure. After the pedal is released, the brake fluid pressure is released when the engine torque reaches the required engine torque. After the brake is released, if the accelerator pedal is not operated for a certain period of time, the brake fluid pressure is automatically released.
